Apple Tree. Private property. Access: Private. Falling Fruit season: August-September.

Very old, very large Golden Delicious apple tree in the abandoned parking lot. Hopefully the vines don't eat it up or the city doesn't rip it down. Abundance of ripe fruit varies from year to year. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: July-September.

All along Commercial Street is Blackberry bushes. The city cuts them back about once every two or three years. Sometimes can ripen earlier (July) with good summer sun [2015].

Near railroad, likely recieve some road runoff. Right across the road from a large shed. BE CAREFUL OF TRAFFIC; it's fast and cars can't see you as they crest the hill. Falling Fruit season: June-August.

A mix of blackberry types including the typically forest-grown Evergreen Blackberry. Public and Private (Apartment) land. Fruit ripens earlier (July) with extra summer sun [2015]. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: August-September.

Unknown Pear tree. Tree is located in abandoned lot behind Hotel along the fence. There are some blackberry bushes tangled around it so bring something to snip them back.

Also blackberries, walnut, and what I think are choke cherries grow here. Access: Public
Two (2) Red Cherry Plum tree's. Equally parts sweet, sour, with a slightly tangy skin. Jams and Juice. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: June-August.
A Marbled Red/Yellow Cherry Plum tree. Strangely sweet like a Yellow Cherry but with an extra tart skin. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: July-August.
Yellow Plum Tree. Very sweet, slightly tangy and especially great fresh. Access: Private but overhanging. Falling Fruit season: July-August.
Falling Fruit access: Private but overhanging.

Several plum trees, mid size fruit and rather oval, heavy bearing in July 2015. On an easement between fanno Creek and some apartments. Low hanging, if you're taller than kid-height. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: July-September.
